title: pavonia leucantha
intro: Pavonia leucantha, also known as Rose Mallow or White Sage Mallow, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves and flowers of Pavonia leucantha are used for their medicinal properties, which include soothing and calming effects, as well as their ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, salves, and infusions to take advantage of its health benefits, which include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting skin health.
